subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life – II)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Research Paper
activities_projects.objective: Analyze women’s movements across India and the world
steps: Choose 2–3 women’s movements (one Indian, one global)
Research the historical background, causes, key leaders, and outcomes
Compare and contrast the movements in a 2–3 page paper
Include citations and references
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research skills and global perspective
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research, analysis, clarity, and referencing (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Essay: Gender Stereotypes and Social Change
activities_projects.objective: Examine the impact of gender stereotypes and strategies to challenge them
steps: Identify three major stereotypes from the chapter or community observations
Analyze how they limit opportunities for women
Suggest measures to overcome these barriers
Write a 500-word essay presenting arguments with examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and analytical writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Argument clarity, depth, examples, and conclusion (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Documentary/Presentation Project
materials: Presentation software or video recording tools
steps: Select a topic (e.g., women in politics, education, or social reform)
Collect images, videos, and interviews if possible
Create a 5–7 minute presentation or documentary highlighting achievements and challenges
Present to class or record and submit
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop multimedia presentation and communication skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Content accuracy, creativity, clarity, and presentation (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analytical Timeline: Evolution of Women’s Rights
activities_projects.objective: Examine the historical progression of women’s rights in India
steps: Create a detailed timeline from 19th century to present
Include legislation, social movements, education reforms, and leadership milestones
Write short explanations of key events and their impact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand historical development and continuity of women’s rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Timeline completeness, accuracy, and analytical notes (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate and Policy Proposal
activities_projects.objective: Develop solutions for contemporary gender issues
steps: Research a current issue affecting women (e.g., wage gap, safety, education access)
Hold a structured class debate on causes and consequences
Prepare a policy proposal suggesting solutions to address the issue
Present the proposal with supporting arguments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students link knowledge with real-world problem solving
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, argument quality, and feasibility of proposal (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interview and Case Study Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Study experiences of women leaders and social reformers
steps: Interview a woman leader in community, education, or activism
Document her experiences, challenges, and strategies for success
Compare findings with examples from the chapter
Prepare a 2–3 page case study with conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical and observational skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Interview depth, analytical comparison, clarity, and presentation (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Survey & Statistical Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Quantify gender equality awareness in the community
steps: Design a survey with 10–15 questions about attitudes toward gender roles
Collect data from 30–50 people in the community
Analyze results statistically and present graphs or charts
Write a short report summarizing findings and insights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply research methodology and data analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Survey design, accuracy, analysis, and insightfulness (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play and Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Simulate key events in women’s movements and analyze outcomes
steps: Select a historical movement or reform initiative from the chapter
Assign roles to students and act out scenarios
Reflect in writing how the movement influenced social change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy, understanding of historical context, and reflective thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Performance, historical accuracy, and depth of reflection (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study: Women in Leadership
activities_projects.objective: Analyze leadership styles of women leaders in different fields
steps: Choose 3 women leaders from politics, science, and social work
Analyze their strategies, decision-making, and challenges
Prepare a comparative table with insights on leadership traits
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand leadership qualities and gender dynamics
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis depth, clarity, and insightfulness (15 marks)

activities_projects.title: Policy Advocacy Campaign
activities_projects.objective: Design a detailed plan to promote gender equality in school or community
steps: Identify a gender-related issue in your community or school
Research possible interventions and best practices
Create a campaign plan with steps, expected outcomes, and awareness strategies
Present the plan to class or submit as a report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop strategic thinking, civic awareness, and planning skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Campaign feasibility, research depth, creativity, and presentation (15 marks)
